title: 10 best herbal creams for hyperthyroidism

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggul creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain the resin of Commiphora mukul, a plant that has been used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to treat various thyroid disorders. The cream's active compounds, such as boswellic acid and guggulsterones, help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune response, thereby alleviating hyperthyroidism symptoms like rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Additionally, guggul creams may also help regulate thyroid hormone production and normalize TSH levels, promoting overall well-being and reducing the risk of complications associated with untreated hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Guggulsterones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce thyroid inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.

Eicosanoids: Guggul extracts contain eicosanoids, such as leukotriene B4, which are known to modulate the immune response and may help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by reducing inflammatory mediators.

Phenolic acids: The phenolic acid content in guggul cream has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help protect against thyroid damage caused by excess thyroid hormones.
remedy_parts: Roots: Guggul roots are rich in bioactive compounds, including guggulu steroids and terpenoids, which have been shown to help regulate thyroid hormone levels and reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the guggul plant contain a high concentration of glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and calm inflamed tissues associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried commiphora mukul resin into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered resin with 20 grams of coconut oil and 5 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts completely.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before whipping the cream into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer.
Transfer the cream to a sterile container and store in the refrigerator at 40°f (4°c) for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they offer a natural and gentle way to soothe the skin and calm the body. The cream's active ingredients, such as ashwagandha extract and other herbal compounds, work together to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. By targeting the skin and nervous system, these creams can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism like anxiety, insomnia, and hot flashes, providing relief from the constant discomfort associated with this condition. Regular use can also promote a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic and Stress-Reducing Properties: Ashwagandha creams can help alleviate anxiety and stress, which are common symptoms of hyperthyroidism, by promoting relaxation and reducing cortisol levels.

Adaptogenic and Antioxidant Properties: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties can help regulate the body's response to stress and inflammation, while its antioxidant properties can help reduce oxidative damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone production in hyperthyroidism.

Anti-Inflammatory and Immune-Modulating Properties: Ashwagandha creams may help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system, which can alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, heat intolerance, and joint pain associated with hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in withanolides, a compound that helps to regulate thyroid hormone levels and reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.

Rhyzomes: Containing bioactive compounds like withaferin A, which has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, useful for soothing skin irritation caused by hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ashwagandha roots extract of 500mg from organic sources.
Combine the extract with coconut oil 100ml and beeswax 20g in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until smooth consistency achieved.
Allow the mixture to cool down to room temperature then add vitamin e oil 10ml and aloe vera gel 10ml.
Pour the final product into sterilized containers and store in a cool dark place for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain rosmarinic acid, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce thyroid hormone production. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties can also soothe symptoms such as heat intolerance and anxiety, common side effects of an overactive thyroid gland. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects on the nervous system may help alleviate restlessness and insomnia, allowing for a better quality of life.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm cream's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety, palpitations, and weight loss.

GABA-boosting properties: Lemon balm contains compounds that increase the levels of GABA (gamma-aminobutyric acid), a neurotransmitter that helps regulate sleep, anxiety, and stress responses, which can be beneficial for hyperthyroidism patients who often experience insomnia and anxiety.

Adrenal soothing properties: The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream may help soothe the adrenal glands, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as hypertension, tachycardia, and nervousness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon balm contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that help to calm and soothe the thyroid gland, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism.

Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried melissa officinalis leaves from a reputable source.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20g of the melissa officinalis powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler setup.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the oil is infused with the herb's properties.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discard solids and let cool to room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain extracts of Adiantum, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the thyroid gland. The creams work by reducing inflammation and swelling in the thyroid tissue, which can help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and anxiety. Additionally, Adiantum's antioxidant properties help protect the thyroid from oxidative stress, promoting overall thyroid health and function. By addressing underlying inflammation and oxidative damage, maidenhair tree creams may offer a natural solution for managing hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_properties: Adaptonine: Helps to regulate thyroid function by reducing the production of thyroid hormones, thus alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
Adenosine: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the thyroid gland and alleviate symptoms such as pain and discomfort associated with hyperthyroidism.

Gallocatechin: Acts as a natural antioxidant, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, potentially reducing the severity of hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, helping to reduce symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety and insomnia.
Barks: Containing bioactive compounds that help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism, including rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Combine the ground ginkgo biloba powder with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax melts and forms a smooth consistency.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping with an electric mixer until light and fluffy.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that inhibits the production of cortisol, a hormone produced by the adrenal glands. Elevated cortisol levels are often associated with hyperthyroidism, as it can exacerbate thyroid hormone imbalances. By reducing cortisol levels, licorice creams may help alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and weight loss. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, a common symptom of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Inhibits the production of thyroid hormones by reducing the activity of the enzyme, thyroid peroxidase, which helps to regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and tremors.

Flavonoids: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, thereby alleviating symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as heat intolerance, sweating, and weight loss.

Amino acids: Specifically, the amino acid, glycyrrhizinic acid, helps to regulate the immune system's response to thyroid hormones, preventing overactivity and subsequent hyperthyroidism symptoms like anxiety, insomnia, and muscle weakness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin, making it beneficial for hyperthyroidism-related skin issues.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the licorice plant are rich in triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as redness and itching.
Barks: Licorice barks contain flavonoids and polyphenols that help to calm skin irritations and reduce inflammation, making them useful for soothing skin affected by hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Remove the heating source and allow the mixture to cool and infuse for 4 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl, discarding the solids and retaining the infused coconut oil for use in the cream.
Blend the infused coconut oil with 200 grams of shea butter, 20 grams of beeswax, and 2 milliliters of vitamin e oil to create a smooth and stable cream preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they support the body's natural detoxification process, which is often impaired in individuals with hyperthyroidism. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and taraxin, have been shown to stimulate liver function, enhance elimination of toxins, and reduce inflammation. By promoting the removal of excess hormones and toxins from the bloodstream, dandelion creams can help alleviate symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, including weight loss, anxiety, and fatigue. Regular use may also help regulate thyroid hormone production and improve overall thyroid health.
remedy_properties: Choleretic properties: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate bile production helps to remove excess thyroid hormones from the body, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and anxiety.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often exacerbated by hyperthyroidism, and also protect against oxidative stress caused by excessive thyroid hormone production.

Detoxifying and purifying properties: Dandelion cream's ability to stimulate liver function and promote detoxification helps to remove toxins and excess hormones from the body, reducing symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as weight loss, insomnia, and irritability.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Contain prenylated flavonoids that have been shown to exhibit anti-thyroid activity, which can help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh dandelion roots to obtain high-quality dried taraxacum officinale powder for later use.
Combine 250 grams of dried taraxacum officinale powder with 500 milliliters of boiling distilled water in a large glass mixing bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes before straining it through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Mix one part of the strained liquid with three parts of unscented coconut oil and add two drops of lavender essential oil.
Whip the mixture in a stand mixer for 10 minutes until it thickens to a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they have a unique ability to block the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can exacerbate thyroid symptoms. The cream's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties also help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate symptoms such as heat intolerance, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat. Additionally, saw palmetto has been shown to regulate thyroid hormone production and promote overall thyroid health, making it a valuable natural supplement for individuals suffering from hyperthyroidism.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Saw palmetto cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as fatty acids and flavonoids, can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, which is often exacerbated by hyperthyroidism.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in saw palmetto cream can neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid cells and contribute to hyperthyroidism symptoms, promoting a healthier thyroid environment.

5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit the enzyme 5-alpha-reductase can help reduce the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), which is a known contributor to hyperthyroidism-related issues like hair loss and acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant contain bioactive compounds that help reduce thyroid hormone production, making it beneficial for treating hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: Saw palmetto seeds are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help soothe inflammation and improve skin health, making them effective for topical application in medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh serenoa repens leaves and dry them according to proper herbal medicine guidelines for two weeks.
Grind dried serenoa repens leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle, weighing 20 grams.
Combine powdered serenoa repens with 100 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax fully melts and the mixture reaches 160 degrees fahrenheit.
Allow the mixture to cool slightly before pouring it into sterilized containers, letting it set at room temperature for two hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain isoflavones, specifically biochanin A and formononetin, which have been shown to bind to thyroid hormone receptors, reducing the production of thyroid hormones. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and nervousness associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, red clover creams may also help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy balance of hormones. As a result, many individuals have reported improved overall well-being and symptom relief when using herbal red clover creams to support their hyperthyroidism treatment plan.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover creams contain isoflavones, which have been shown to help reduce thyroid hormone levels and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to T3, the more active form of thyroid hormone.

Phytoestrogens: The phytoestrogens present in red clover creams may help counteract the effects of excess thyroid hormones by binding to estrogen receptors and reducing the production of thyroid-stimulating hormones.
Antioxidants: Red clover creams contain antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can exacerbate hyperthyroidism symptoms such as fatigue, weight loss, and anxiety.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, red clover roots are believed to help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: Containing isoflavones, red clover seeds have been shown to help regulate thyroid hormone levels and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers and leaves in amounts of approximately 10 grams.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels for several hours.
Grind the dried trifolium pratense into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground trifolium pratense with sweet almond oil and beeswax at a ratio of 10:5:1 for approximately 30 minutes until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow it to cool and solidify completely, which may take several hours or overnight depending on temperature and humidity.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that soothe and calm the thyroid gland. The root of the burdock plant is rich in fiber, vitamins, and minerals that support adrenal function and promote a healthy metabolism. Additionally, burdock's ability to cleanse the body of toxins and heavy metals can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hyperthyroidism, thereby supporting overall thyroid health and promoting a balanced bodily response.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of burdock root, particularly its ability to neutralize free radicals, can help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive thyroid hormone production.
Thyroid-regulating property: Burdock root has been traditionally used to regulate thyroid function and reduce symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism, such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help reduce inflammation and soothe symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the conversion of thyroid hormones and reduce hyperthyroid symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried arctium lappa roots and st john's wort flowers in equal proportions of 2 tablespoons each.
Combine the herbal mixture with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass jar and discard the solids after 15 minutes.
Add a few drops of lavender essential oil to enhance relaxation and reduce anxiety after 1 hour.
Fill sterilized jars with the cream and store them in the refrigerator for at least 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain a calming compound that reduces stress and anxiety, which are common symptoms of this condition. The cream's active ingredients work to soothe the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing feelings of jitteriness and restlessness. Additionally, valerian has been shown to help regulate thyroid hormone production, thereby alleviating hyperthyroidism's characteristic symptoms such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss. Sedative effect: Valerian's sedative properties can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, which may be disrupted by the symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as insomnia, restlessness, and anxiety.

remedy_recipe: Harvest valeriana officinalis roots in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm dark place for two weeks or use a dehydrator on low heat for 12 hours.

Grind dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle and measure out 10 grams of powder per 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil.

Combine the ground valeriana officinalis root powder with the carrier oil in a double boiler setup over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the mixture reaches a temperature of 65°c to 70°c.
Strain the cooled herbal cream through cheesecloth into sterilized jars and store in the refrigerator at a temperature of 4°c to 8°c for up to six months or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain menthol, a natural compound that can help reduce symptoms of an overactive thyroid gland. Menthol has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can alleviate muscle weakness, tremors, and anxiety commonly experienced by those with hyperthyroidism. remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Peppermint's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by thyroid dysfunction, which can alleviate symptoms like fatigue and weight loss.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling in the thyroid gland and surrounding tissues, potentially alleviating pain and discomfort associated with hyperthyroidism.

Thyroid-regulating properties: Some studies suggest that peppermint's phytochemicals may have a regulatory effect on thyroid hormone production, helping to normalize thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain flavonoids and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them effective in reducing thyroid inflammation and promoting relaxation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves in early morning misty conditions for optimal potency retention.
Combine 20g of dried peppermint leaves with 100ml of coconut oil and 50ml of olive oil in a double boiler.
Gently simmer the mixture for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, to infuse the herbs into the oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the infused oil to cool and solidify at room temperature before whipping with a hand mixer until smooth.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with hyperthyroidism because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antithyroid properties. The antioxidants present in thyme cream help reduce inflammation caused by thyroid hormones, which can alleviate symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and rapid heartbeat associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, the thymol content in thyme cream has been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid hormones, helping to regulate thyroid function and provide relief from hyperthyroidism symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a trusted source or purchase them from a reputable supplier weighing approximately one ounce.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder taking about 10 minutes.
Combine the ground thymus vulgaris powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler heating mixture for 30-40 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool until it reaches a temperature of around 140°f then whip with an electric mixer until creamy and smooth taking about 10 minutes.
Transfer the cream to glass jars filled to the top and store in a cool dark place at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with hyperthyroidism because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which in turn can help to reduce symptoms such as anxiety, insomnia, and restlessness that are commonly associated with hyperthyroidism. The anti-inflammatory properties of chamomile also help to soothe and calm irritated skin and mucous membranes, providing relief from skin conditions such as eczema and acne that are often exacerbated by thyroid hormone imbalances. Additionally, chamomile's natural antibacterial properties may help to reduce the risk of infection in cases where hyperthyroidism leads to compromised immune function.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered matricaria chamomilla with 20 grams of beeswax, 30 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches 120°f to 140°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 100°f to 110°f before whipping it with an electric whisk or spatula for about 10 minutes until it thickens.
Pour the cooled and whipped cream mixture into glass jars, store them in a cool dark place, and use within six months as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with hyperthyroidism because they contain curcumin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that has been shown to reduce symptoms of the condition. Curcumin has been found to suppress the production of thyroid hormones, which can help alleviate symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, weight loss, and heat intolerance. remedy_recipe: Dry curcuma longa roots for 2 days in a warm dry place until they reach a dark brown color.
Grind 100 grams of dried curcuma longa roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix the powdered curcuma longa with 200 grams of sweet almond oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at a low temperature for 30 minutes or until the beeswax melts completely.
Allow the cream to cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it into sterilized glass jars.

The unique compounds present in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to inhibit the production of thyroid-stimulating hormones (TSH), which can help regulate an overactive thyroid gland. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cinnamomum verum bark and dry it in a warm oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Combine 10 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark with 100 milliliters of olive oil and let it steep for 24 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and combine the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of coconut oil, and 5 milliliters of vitamin e oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f until the beeswax is fully melted, stirring occasionally for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Measure out 20 grams of dried panax ginseng root powder and add it to a glass bowl.
Combine 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Add 5 grams of vitamin e oil and 2 grams of aloe vera gel to the warm mixture and stir well.
Pour the final mixture into glass jars and refrigerate it for 30 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams help with hyperthyroidism because they contain hyperforin, a natural compound that has been shown to decrease thyroid hormone levels. The cream works by binding to specific receptors in the body, which helps to reduce the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3). This can lead to a reduction in symptoms such as anxiety, rapid heartbeat, and weight loss associated with hyperthyroidism. Additionally, St John's Wort cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate joint pain and swelling that often accompanies this condition.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to inhibit the conversion of T4 to T3, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as rapid heartbeat and weight loss.

Hypotensive properties: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort have been found to exhibit vasodilatory effects, which can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism-induced hypertension.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant compounds in St John's Wort cream may help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism, which can contribute to tissue damage and other complications.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of bioactive compounds, roots are used to create creams that help regulate thyroid hormone production and reduce inflammation associated with hyperthyroidism.

Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and hyperforin, the flowers are processed into creams that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism such as anxiety and insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves in the early morning dew-covered state with scissors for 100g.
Dry the harvested leaves in a single layer using air circulation method at room temperature for 24 hours.
Grind dried hypericum perforatum leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix ground hypericum perforatum powder with sweet almond oil at a ratio of 1:2 and heat at low temperature for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and store in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams help with hyperthyroidism because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) levels and alleviate symptoms of hyperthyroidism. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe the discomfort associated with thyroid-related inflammation. Additionally, feverfew's ability to stabilize blood sugar levels can aid in managing the metabolic changes caused by an overactive thyroid gland. By applying the cream topically, individuals with hyperthyroidism may experience relief from symptoms such as heat intolerance, anxiety, and rapid heartbeat.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Feverfew creams contain parthenolide, a compound that has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with hyperthyroidism.
Antioxidant activity: Feverfew contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that can exacerbate hyperthyroidism symptoms.

Calcitonin-stimulating properties: Feverfew has been shown to stimulate the production of calcitonin, a hormone that helps regulate calcium levels in the body, which is particularly important for individuals with hyperthyroidism who may experience bone loss and increased risk of osteoporosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe symptoms of hyperthyroidism.
Buds: Feverfew buds contain parthenolide, a compound that has been shown to reduce thyroid hormone production and alleviate hyperthyroidism symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh tanacetum parthenium leaves, ensuring they are free of pesticides and other contaminants.
Dry the leaves using a food dehydrator at 150°f (65°c) for 6 hours or until crumbly to preserve potency.
Grind 100g dried tanacetum parthenium leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 20g of the powdered tanacetum parthenium with 80g of sweet almond oil in a small saucepan, heating gently over low heat.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 24 hours before straining and bottling the resulting cream in dark glass containers.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for hyperthyroidism are topical preparations made from natural herbs and plant extracts that help alleviate symptoms associated with an overactive thyroid gland. These creams work by reducing inflammation, calming anxiety, and soothing skin irritation often experienced by individuals with hyperthyroidism. For instance, creams containing ashwagandha, guggul, and licorice root have shown to reduce symptoms such as heat intolerance, anxiety, and sleep disturbances. By using these herbal creams, individuals can experience improved quality of life, increased energy levels, and reduced stress, ultimately leading to a better management of their hyperthyroidism.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hyperthyroidism with herbal creams is to combine a blend of calming herbs such as passionflower, chamomile, and lemon balm to soothe the thyroid gland. Additionally, incorporating anti-inflammatory herbs like turmeric and ginger helps reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Apply topically 2-3 times a day, massaging gently into the skin to promote absorption and relaxation.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hyperthyroidism that are treated with herbal creams are often related to thyroid dysfunction or hormonal imbalances. For example, herbal creams may be used to treat hypothyroidism, adrenal fatigue, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS), which can present with symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and mood changes. These herbal creams often contain ingredients like ashwagandha, bladderwrack, and saw palmetto, which are believed to support thyroid function and hormone balance.
